All right, so in a level 3 campaign, what we do is we take an audience plus ad combo. In this case, let's say the audience wealth and that super simple image ad, like that crushed, right? And we create a singular campaign around that one audience. And we use a different type of bidding on that one. All right. And so the typical way that most Facebook ads are run is kind of a way where you're saying, all right, Facebook, just, you know, whatever, get me the lowest costs and cheapest conversions you can and go to town. What we're going to do is we're going to use something called cost cap bidding, which is where we're basically saying, all right, Facebook, here's how much I'm willing to pay per customer within this audience. And we're going to take the same audience and we're going to run four different bids at the same time. Some of them are going to work and some of them are not. All right.
